title: Bouncing Ball
description: Interactive simulation demonstrating motion, velocity, and collision detection, with a Start/Pause control that emits a single xAPI `experienced` statement carrying the elapsed run time. Serves as the reference emitter for the Start/Pause dwell pattern in the xAPI Producer Contract v1.
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Physics
Motion
Velocity
Collision Detection
Kinematics
learningObjectives: Observe how speed affects the motion of a bouncing ball
Predict where the ball will travel after a collision with a boundary
Explain why a Start/Pause interval is the natural unit of engagement evidence for a simulation

keyQuestions: Why does pressing Start emit no xAPI statement at all?
A student starts the simulation and closes the tab without pausing. What is emitted, and why?
Why does the speed slider produce a different object_type than the Start/Pause button?
Why is the ball paused when the page loads instead of already moving?
